classification_scheme.a: Third-party paying customer deployment with measurable $/token outcome
classification_scheme.b: Operator (project author or close collaborator) deployment with real logs / accounting
classification_scheme.c: Modeled / analytical scenario authored as a reference case
classification_scheme.d: Marketing narrative without a specific deployment

notes: All committed cases below are class (b) or (c). No class (a) row exists yet.
Class (c) rows are reproducible via the benchmark_*.py scripts and are not a substitute for class (a).
When the first class (a) row lands, bump external_paying_customer_count and update the PRODUCTION_EVIDENCE.md disclosure.

cases.workload_class: Daily token/cache budget digest + optional prune
cases.before_summary: Prompt-loop agent re-invoked daily for thresholds, prune policy, digest, notify
cases.after_summary: Compiled AINL graph via bridge; deterministic adapter execution

cases.workload_class: User-facing LLM traffic (chat, tools, recurring tasks)
cases.before_summary: Equivalent prompt-loop agent architecture on paid Claude Opus
cases.after_summary: AINL compile-once workflows + free-tier model routing
cases.caveat: Mixes model-choice savings (free tier) with architectural efficiency. Not an isolated A/B of a single cron job. See PRODUCTION_EVIDENCE.md Case 2.

cases.workload_class: HTTP health check every 5 min; LLM alert on incident only
cases.host: ainl run / runner / ArmaraOS scheduled Hand
cases.before_summary: Modeled prompt-loop: 2 LLM orchestration calls/run even when healthy
cases.after_summary: Compiled AINL: 0 LLM tokens on healthy polls (~90% of runs)
cases.baseline_qualifier: vs baseline A (prompt-loop). Not valid vs baseline B (hand-written runner).
